title: nageia bracteata
intro: Nageia bracteata, also known as the Nageia tree, is a species of coniferous tree that belongs to the Podocarpaceae family. Traditionally, the bark and leaves of this plant have been used in folk medicine, particularly in the Pacific region, for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Nageia bracteata are attributed to its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and treat wounds. In herbal preparations, the bark and leaves are often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness the plant's medicinal properties.
